prompts: Thermal expansion and contraction occur when a material experiences a temperature change. When the temperature rises, the material's particles gain kinetic energy and move apart, causing the material to expand. Conversely, when the temperature drops, particle motion decreases, and the material contracts as particles move closer together. This dimensional shift varies with material properties, as some materials expand or contract more than others due to their coefficient of thermal expansion. These changes are especially critical in engineering and construction, where precise measurements can be altered by temperature fluctuations.
